summary: PayPal checkout flow (backend + frontend) verified end-to-end on the preview URL. Backend: 13/13 pytest passed (test_paypal_flow.py) including POST /api/payments/create-paypal-order (200 with real approve link), GET /api/payments/paypal-return (200 HTML containing koala-mining://paypal/success deep link), GET /api/payments/paypal-cancel (200 HTML containing koala-mining://paypal/cancel deep link), and all 6 loadAppData() regression endpoints (/api/auth/me, /api/wallet/balance, /api/wallet/status, /api/miners/list, /api/store/miners, /api/referrals/stats). Admin gating: /api/admin/users is 200 for admin and 403 (correctly fixed from iteration_1) for non-admin. Frontend: logged in as koalatest@example.com, dashboard loaded with all loadAppData calls firing (confirmed in network log), navigated to Store (9 miners listed), tapped 'Purchase & Activate' on Standard Miner ($7.99), confirmed via 'Pay with PayPal' → POST /api/payments/create-paypal-order returned 200 with approve link 'https://www.paypal.com/checkoutnow?token=2C265409TK593900L', Linking.openURL fired (window.open intercepted) and the '🌐 PayPal Checkout Opened' custom alert displayed exactly as designed. Did NOT complete real payment. No red screens / no page crashes. Recurring non-fatal RN-Web console error 'Unexpected text node: )}. A text node cannot be a child of a <View>' is STILL PRESENT (carried over from iteration_1) and a stray ')}' is even rendered visually under the Premium Miners section on the dashboard.

minor.issue: Broad `except Exception` at server.py:2806 swallows HTTPException(404 'Miner not found') and re-raises as 500. Add `except HTTPException: raise` before the broad except (same pattern as iteration_1 fix on /api/admin/users at ~3996). Currently returns 500 for unknown miner_id instead of 404.

ui_bugs.issue: Recurring console error 'Unexpected text node: )}. A text node cannot be a child of a <View>' fires ~20 times per render AND a literal ')}' is rendered on the dashboard between 'Premium Miners (0)' and the bottom tab bar. Persists from iteration_1 and was NOT fixed. A misplaced fragment closer or stray expression inside a <View>. Reference: /app/frontend/app/premium-mining-app.tsx (file is 4884 lines; grep for ')}' followed by JSX siblings inside a <View>; most likely near the Premium Miners section in the Dashboard render block).

Hash rates displayed on the Store page do NOT match the hardcoded miners list used by /api/payments/create-paypal-order (server.py:2707-2717). E.g., Ultimate Miner UI shows 10.0 TH/s for $449.99 but backend create-paypal-order list has miner_6th = 6000 GH/s for $449.99. Legendary shows 15.0 TH/s for $749.99 but backend has 8000 GH/s for $599.99 (miner_8th). Prices and IDs likely diverge between /api/store/miners (frontend source) and the hardcoded list in create-paypal-order. User will be charged the create-paypal-order price, which may not match the Store-displayed price. Verify both lists are sourced from one constant.
Still no testID props anywhere in premium-mining-app.tsx (Purchase & Activate buttons, Pay with PayPal button, modal Cancel buttons, tab bar). Reproduces iteration_1 finding.

issues: After login a 'Success! 🎉 Welcome back to Koala Mining!' alert AND a separate 'Advertisement Required' modal both stack on top of the Dashboard, requiring two dismissals to interact with the app. Consider chaining or auto-dismissing the welcome alert.

react_native_issues.issue: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow), 'textShadow*' deprecated (use textShadow), 'props.pointerEvents' deprecated (use style.pointerEvents). Multiple occurrences. Non-blocking but should be migrated. Reference: /app/frontend/app/premium-mining-app.tsx (multiple lines).
'[expo-notifications] Listening to push token changes is not yet fully supported on web' - expected on web preview, no action needed.

handleDeepLink is also invoked with the regular web URL on page load (console log 'Deep link received: https://admin-balance-mgmt-1.preview.emergentagent.com/'). It correctly no-ops because the URL doesn't contain 'paypal/success' or 'paypal/cancel', but the substring match `url.includes('paypal/success')` could false-positive if the app's own URL ever contains 'paypal/success' as a path segment. Consider exact-prefix match on 'koala-mining://paypal/' instead of substring. Ref: premium-mining-app.tsx:597-614.

action_items: Fix POST /api/payments/create-paypal-order to re-raise HTTPException before broad except (server.py:2806) so unknown miner returns 404 instead of 500.
RESOLVE the stray ')}' / 'Unexpected text node' bug in premium-mining-app.tsx near the Premium Miners section of the Dashboard render. Visible to end users on the dashboard.

Reconcile the Store miner list (frontend / /api/store/miners) with the hardcoded miners list in create-paypal-order (server.py:2707-2717) - hash rates and at least one price disagree (Legendary $749.99 in UI vs $599.99 in backend). Source both from one shared constant to prevent user-facing price/spec mismatches.
Tighten the deep-link substring match in premium-mining-app.tsx (use scheme prefix koala-mining://paypal/ instead of url.includes('paypal/success')).
Add testID props to Store cards, Purchase & Activate, Pay with PayPal, Cancel Purchase, and tab bar items to enable automated testing.
Auto-dismiss or suppress the 'Welcome back' success alert when the next-screen modal (Ad Required) is also queued.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:2806 broad `except Exception` swallows HTTPException(404) - identical anti-pattern as iteration_1 found in /api/admin/users. Hunt down all `except Exception` blocks that follow `raise HTTPException(...)` and add `except HTTPException: raise` first.
premium-mining-app.tsx is now 4884 lines (was 4841). The recurring 'Unexpected text node ')}' indicates fragile rendering near a Dashboard <View> - smaller modules would help isolate.
Two sources of truth for the miners catalog: /api/store/miners and the inline list inside create_paypal_order. Should be a single STORE_MINERS constant imported by both.

Frontend uses substring url.includes('paypal/success') to detect deep links - safer to check `url.startsWith('koala-mining://paypal/success')` to avoid false positives if the web URL ever contains that substring.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for tested flow (login -> store -> PayPal init)

seed_data_creation: None for this iteration. Test reuses koalatest@example.com / colourfulkoaladevelopment@gmail.com from /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PayPal end-to-end on web preview: backend create-paypal-order accepts {miner_id: 'miner_100gh', promo_code: '', subscription_type: 'one_time'} for koalatest and returns 200 with order_id + 4 links incl. rel=approve to live PayPal (LIVE mode in .env). To intercept the Linking.openURL call in headless Playwright, monkey-patch window.open BEFORE clicking 'Pay with PayPal' - Expo Linking on web routes through window.open. /api/payments/paypal-return is reachable with ANY token (returns HTML deep-link page even for FAKE token), so tests don't need a real PayPal capture. /api/admin/users non-admin gating is now 403 (was 500 in iteration_1, FIXED).
rca_of_issue: PayPal end-to-end charged but no auto-redirect / no instant miner activation: This session focused only on the FRONTEND hardening (AppState foreground listener + paypalPendingRef.current to retrigger loadAppData on return). Verified that (a) paypalPendingRef.current = true is set immediately before Linking.openURL(approvalUrl) at premium-mining-app.tsx:1254, (b) AppState 'change' listener calls loadAppData() when state becomes 'active' && paypalPendingRef.current (lines 629-634), (c) the deep-link 'paypal/success' handler also calls loadAppData() + clears the ref (lines 597-605). All wired correctly. The actual deep-link bounce-back and AppState foreground transition CANNOT be exercised in a web preview (no koala-mining:// scheme handler on the browser) - must be validated on a physical device or APK build. Backend captures + activates the miner inside the paypal-return handler so server-side activation is independent of the deep link firing.
